business.headline: Mukesh Ambani's Jio IPO is here: Reliance to offer up to 27 crore fresh sharesMukesh Ambani announced at Reliance's 49th AGM that Jio Platforms will file its DRHP with Sebi today. The move formally starts the long-awaited listing process and adds momentum to India's pipeline of mega public issues.

Sensex ends 600 points lower, Nifty tops 24,000; IT stocks bleedThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 607.08 points, or 0.78%, to close at 76,802.90, while the NSE Nifty50 shed 155.05 points, or 0.64%, to settle at 24,013.10.

Mukesh Ambani announces Jio IPO, draft papers to be filed with Sebi todayAddressing shareholders at Reliance Industries' 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM), Ambani said Jio Platforms' draft red herring prospectus (DRHP) has been approved by the company's board and will be filed with market regulator Sebi later today.

Government grants copyright society status to PPL India, boosting royaltiesThe government has registered PPL India as a Copyright Society under the Copyright Act, 1957. The move gives music users a clearer licensing route and is expected to improve royalty payments to rights holders and artists.

Why Infosys took the biggest hit after Accenture's warningAccenture reported strong AI demand but trimmed its growth outlook, signalling that clients are still holding back on spending. The warning hammered Indian IT stocks, with Infosys leading the rout.

Jio IPO moves ahead as Reliance bets big on AI and new energyRIL AGM 2026: Jio Platforms to file IPO papers with Sebi; Mukesh Ambani unveils five major value creation pathways, bets big on AI, energy self-reliance, satellite connectivity, media, FMCG and global expansion

Sensex down 700 points: Why is stock market falling today after a 5-day rally?The S&P BSE Sensex was down 635.85 points, or 0.84%, at 76,774.13, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 170.10 points, or 0.70%, to 23,997.90 as of 10:13 am. The decline snapped a strong rally that had seen the Sensex gain nearly 4.8% and the Nifty rise over 4.3% in the previous five sessions.

Reliance AGM: Will Mukesh Ambani finally reveal the Jio IPO date today?Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani is set to address shareholders at the company's 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM) today, and the biggest question on Dalal Street is simple: Will he finally announce a timeline for the Jio IPO?

Accenture warning sends Infosys, TCS and HCLTech tumbling. Here's whyMajor Indian IT stocks fell sharply after Accenture lowered the upper end of its annual revenue growth forecast. The sell-off has renewed concerns over weak discretionary spending, slower deal conversion and the pace of demand recovery.

Sensex, Nifty tumble as Accenture warning triggers IT crash on Dalal StreetInfosys, TCS and other technology stocks tumbled after the global IT giant flagged slower growth, dragging the broader market lower despite falling oil prices.

India's Swiss bank funds fall 8%, while direct deposits jump 50%Swiss National Bank data showed total funds linked to Indian clients fell in 2025 even as direct customer deposits rose sharply. The figures underline that Indian-linked assets in Switzerland are not the same as black money estimates and reflect a more transparent banking regime.

Jio IPO, AI, green energy: 5 big questions Mukesh Ambani may answer at RIL AGMMukesh Ambani is set to face shareholder questions on Jio's IPO timeline, AI plans and green energy projects at Reliance's 49th AGM. With key deadlines nearing, investors are seeking proof that earlier commitments are moving into execution.

Got your Form 16? Check these 6 details before filing your ITRSince Form 16 forms the foundation of income reporting for salaried taxpayers, spending a few minutes verifying the information can save you a lot of trouble later.

The third dimension – after inspectors and employers, now the consultantsIn the eleventh part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines the role of labour consultants who, instead of ensuring compliance, sometimes help employers create the appearance of compliance while facilitating violations of labour laws.

NSE IPO filing could unlock Rs 25,000 crore for SBI, Temasek and top investorsNSE has filed draft papers for its long-delayed initial public offering after nearly a decade. The move could unlock major gains for existing investors and value the exchange at about $57 billion.

8th Pay Commission: How fitment factors from 2.57 to 3.83 may change salariesEmployee unions have sought a higher fitment factor as the 8th Pay Commission considers revisions to salaries and pensions. The final multiplier will determine how much basic pay and pensions rise for millions of central government employees and retirees.

Sensex closes 254 points higher, Nifty near 24,200; IndiGo up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 254.36 points, or 0.33%, to close at 77,409.98, while the NSE Nifty50 gained around 82 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 24,168.

Crude oil is no longer the villain. A new threat could raise your household billEconomists say a weak monsoon has overtaken crude oil as India's main inflation risk. Poor rainfall could raise food prices, squeeze household budgets and complicate RBI rate cuts.

EPF accounts to get 8.25% interest soon after government approvalThe government has approved an 8.25% EPF interest rate for 2025-26, paving the way for account credits this month. The decision lets EPFO begin processing payments for over 7 crore subscribers after Finance Ministry clearance.

Why the Rs 30,000-crore NSE IPO is unlike any other IPOFor nearly 10 years, the NSE IPO was the market's longest-running "coming soon" story. Now, India's biggest exchange is finally ready to test its value in public.

entertainment.headline: Balan The Boy review: The year's most moving mother-son story disguised as thrillerRelease Date:Fri Jun 19, 2026 Balan The Boy Review: Chidambaram's film follows the journey of a mother and son who survive by constantly changing identities. The thriller deepens into a restrained study of love, fear and sacrifice.

Shilpa Shinde calls out 'mafia' TV producers over 90-day payment delays Actor Shilpa Shinde accused television producers of operating like a mafia and withholding actors' payments for up to 90 days. Her remarks, made while reacting to actor Shehzada Dhami's dues claim, renewed focus on fear, pressure and lack of support within the industry.

Vikram Bhatt reacts to Haunted 3D beating Main Vaapas Aaunga at box office Vikram Bhatt's Haunted 3D: Echoes of the Past has moved ahead of Main Vaapas Aaunga at the box office. Bhatt said targeted promotion and the franchise's hidden audience shaped the film's run.

Rajkumar Hirani shuts down '4th Idiot' buzz, confirms 3 Idiots sequel is in works Filmmaker Rajkumar Hirani confirmed that a 3 Idiots sequel is being written and said no new character is joining the trio. His remarks shut down the 4 Idiots chatter while signalling a story set 20 years after the original.

Maa Inti Bangaaram review: Guns, sickles, sarees and Samantha owning everythingRelease Date:Fri Jun 19, 2026 Maa Inti Bangaaram movie review: Director Nandini Reddy's Maa Inti Bangaaram, starring Samantha Ruth Prabhu and Gulshan Devaiah, is a female-led commercial film with great action. It is also a film that proves women can headline an action drama and do it convincingly.

Hyderabad court summons Allu Arjun as accused in Sandhya Theatre stampede case Nampally Court has summoned Allu Arjun to appear on June 22 in the Sandhya Theatre stampede case. The hearing comes as the injured boy's family continues to cope with long-term treatment and ongoing support.

Maa Inti Bangaaram X review: Samantha shines, everything else is hit-and-miss Early reactions to Maa Inti Bangaaram on X were positive to mixed on the day of its release. While many viewers called the Samantha starter a family entertainer, others felt the writing and emotional impact fell short.

Colony review: A smart, messy and inventive zombie thriller by Yeon Sang-hoRelease Date:Fri Jun 19, 2026 Colony movie review: Yeon Sang-ho's Colony turns a sealed Seoul outbreak into a battle against evolving infected. Its bold ideas and unsettling atmosphere stand out, even as the human drama thins.

Cocktail 2 review: Modern love, loyalty test and script that belongs on reality TVRelease Date:Fri Jun 19, 2026 Cocktail 2 movie review: The story follows a long-term relationship disrupted by a glamorous new entrant. But the visual polish and big ideas cannot hide the weak emotional stakes in Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna-starrer.

High court issues notice to producer Vipul Shah over Kerala Story 2 title row The Kerala High Court has issued notice before admission to producer Vipul Amrutlal Shah in a plea against Kerala Story 2: Goes Beyond. The court questioned whether the challenge survives after release, while the petitioner pointed to the film's OTT run and wider circulation.

No blanket, 3 am illness: How Samantha helped sister-in-law like a silent warrior Raj Nidimoru's sister Sheetal recalled how actor Samantha looked after her through the night days after her wedding to the filmmaker. Her note, shared before Maa Inti Bangaaram's release, linked that memory to the film's title.

Degenerated society: Imtiaz Ali questions those comfortable with burqa, parda Filmmaker Imtiaz Ali's remarks on burqa, parda and social conditioning during a recent interview have triggered debate online. Here's what the filmmaker said and why the conversation is gaining attention.

Sunil Grover caught sleeping on Ganga ghat in viral video, fans love his simplicity Actor Sunil Grover shared an Instagram video showing himself resting on Ganga ghat at night. The video has gone viral with over 4 million views.

Kala Hiran producer claims 2 death threats within hours from Salman Khan fan Kala Hiran producer Amit Jani has alleged that he received a second death threat within 24 hours after filing an earlier complaint in Jodhpur. He said the latest caller claimed to be a Salman Khan fan, raising security concerns ahead of the film's release.

Cocktail 2 X review: Cheers for Kriti, side-eye for Rashmika's accent Cocktail 2 opened in theatres on June 19 and received mixed reactions on X. Much of the online chatter praised Kriti Sanon, while the screenplay and Rashmika Mandanna's accent faced criticism.

Ranveer Singh's drastic 18-month no-interview pledge amid Don 3 row Ranveer Singh has reportedly decided not to speak on the Don 3 controversy or give interviews for 18 months. The silence comes as his fallout with Farhan Akhtar has widened into a legal dispute ahead of Pralay.

Shraddha Kapoor's Eetha teaser leaked online, stunned fans call it promising as hell The leaked teaser of Eetha shows actor Shraddha Kapoor as tamasha artiste Vithabai Bhau Mang Narayangaonkar during a stage performance around childbirth. The glimpse has gone viral, drawing strong fan reactions and raising buzz around the film.

Karan Kundrra defends seeking parents' nod before Desi Bling proposal to Tejasswi Karan Kundrra has responded to criticism over consulting his parents before proposing to Tejasswi Prakash. His remarks have sparked fresh conversation about family and relationships.

SRK waived Rs 42 lakh for Deool Band 2; Marathi film went on to earn Rs 80 crore Filmmaker Pravin Tarde said Shah Rukh Khan asked Red Chillies Entertainment to waive pending DCP dues for Deool Band 2 before its release. The gesture helped the Marathi film reach cinemas despite a budget crunch and is now being remembered after its box-office success.

general news.headline: Entire Ram Mandir management needs to go: Nripendra Misra flags deep flawsRam Janmabhoomi Temple Construction Committee chairman called for a complete overhaul of the Ram Temple's management system, saying the donation misappropriation controversy has exposed serious failures in supervision and accountability.

Trump claims Meloni begged him for picture at G7 Summit. Here's her responseGiorgia Meloni rejected Donald Trump's claim that she begged for a photograph at the G7 summit. The exchange has deepened strains in a relationship once seen as one of his closest political partnerships in Europe.

Trump names PM Modi among world leaders he admires, calls him very tough cookieDonald Trump praised Narendra Modi again and called him a very tough cookie in an upcoming Axios interview. The remark followed his G7 comments and underscored how he contrasted Modi with Xi Jinping.

No question of forgiveness: BJP vows action after Karnataka cross-voting joltThe Karnataka Legislative Council election exposed cross-voting in the BJP and JD(S) camps as Congress won five of seven seats. The result has triggered disciplinary warnings in the opposition and highlighted internal strains before future contests.

Rajya Sabha results in 3 states expose Congress's puzzling contrastsDespite having the numbers, Congress suffered setbacks in Rajya Sabha contests in both Jharkhand and Madhya Pradesh. Yet, on the same day, it secured 16 extra votes to win comfortably in Karnataka's Legislative Council polls, highlighting stark contrasts in the party's political and alliance management.

Why Dyer was felicitated at Golden Temple days after Jallianwala Bagh massacreDays after the Jallianwala Bagh massacre in 1919, Golden Temple managers invited Brigadier General Reginald Dyer and honoured him with a siropa and kirpan. The priests even urged the 'Butcher of Amritsar' to become a Sikh and agreed to waive the requirement of unshorn hair when he said he could not follow it as a serving British officer.

The last-minute NEET-UG frenzy: Students running one race twiceAs the countdown to the June 21 NEET-UG re-exam enters its final phase, hostels, classrooms, and coaching corridors in Rajasthan's coaching city of Sikar have become arenas of relentless revision and quiet anxiety. Here's what's happening in India's NEET coaching capital.

Man ties up wife, shaves her head, forces children to urinate on her; arrestedA government vehicle driver in Chhattisgarh's Surajpur district was arrested for allegedly subjecting his wife to severe physical and mental torture, including assault, public humiliation, and forcing her to drink urine over suspicions of an affair.

Which tribe is Usha from? Vance shares mother's confusion on wife's Indian rootsJD Vance, in a podcast, recounted how his mother asked about his wife Usha's 'tribe' when he mentioned her Indian roots. Vance said his mother's question came from a lack of familiarity with different cultures.

IIM Ahmedabad professor uses Dhurandhar scene to teach statistics in class. WatchAn IIM Ahmedabad professor used a scene from Dhurandhar to explain statistical concepts such as cluster sampling and stratification. The video went viral as viewers praised the inventive way complex concepts were broken down.

Does China have a caste system or is it a figment of imagination of Indians?China, the world's manufacturing powerhouse and a socialist state governed by a communist party, is being accused online of having a birth-based caste system that shapes access to education, healthcare, and opportunity. Does China really have a caste system? Here's the reality.

Did RJD settle Bihar score with Congress in Jharkhand Rajya Sabha election?Congress leader Pranav Jha's defeat in the Jharkhand Rajya Sabha election has triggered a Congress-RJD blame game. The row has led to questions if the RJD might have sought revenge for the Bihar Rajya Sabha election debacle earlier this year.
Kancheepuram retains top spot in India's infidelity rankingsKancheepuram has again recorded the highest Ashley Madison sign-ups in India for May 2026.

Trump shares 'historian's' note comparing him to Hitler. New book outs truthRecently, Donald Trump posted a two-page document by a 'historian' that compares his influence to some of the most notorious rulers like Hitler and Genghis Khan. However, a new book on Trump has disclosed a startling fact.

Setback for Telegram, court says government's temporary ban isn't disproportionateThe government had imposed the temporary restriction citing apprehensions that Telegram was being used by organised cheating networks allegedly involved in the NEET-UG controversy.

Watch: Truck flees after dumping stones on Noida highway, cops left clearing debrisAn overloaded truck allegedly sped away on the wrong side of the Eastern Peripheral Expressway, dropping stones onto the road. The viral video has drawn scrutiny after Noida transport officials denied involvement and Dadri Police said no complaint was received.

Monsoon stuck: How five weather systems teamed up to stop India's rainsIndia's monsoon advance has stalled after an early onset as multiple weather systems disrupted its flow. The pause has widened rainfall deficits, though forecasters expect conditions to improve in late June.

sports.headline: Delhi Capitals to appoint Yuvraj Singh as batting coach after poor IPL 2026: Report Former India all-rounder Yuvraj Singh is set to join Delhi Capitals as their batting coach for the IPL 2027 season, marking his first coaching role in the league. The move is part of a major support staff overhaul, with Sourav Ganguly expected to take over as Director of Cricket.

'Hunger never dies': Virat Kohli to play for RCB till 2030, says franchise CEO Royal Challengers Bengaluru CEO Rajesh Menon believes Virat Kohli can continue playing until at least 2030, saying the veteran batter's hunger, fitness and passion for success remain undiminished.

Virat, Rohit's 2027 World Cup future not meant for public consumption: BCCI Secretary BCCI secretary Saikia refused to discuss Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's place in India's 2027 World Cup plans, saying such decisions belong in the boardroom. He also said India's bilateral cricket with Bangladesh will be guided strictly by Government of India policy, not political changes there.

Iran to lodge complaint with FIFA over World Cup travel restrictions FIFA World Cup 2026: Iran will complain to FIFA after saying US travel rules disrupted its World Cup preparation. The restrictions have turned a football campaign into a dispute over access, recovery and competitive fairness.

Neeraj Chopra joins anti-doping fight, launches clean sport campaign with IOA Olympic champion Neeraj Chopra has joined hands with the Indian Olympic Association (IOA) to launch the Clean Sports Campaign, an initiative aimed at educating athletes about the dangers of doping and promoting fair play.

Harry Kane receives iconic gift from Ronaldo Nazario, poses with signed Brazil jersey FIFA World Cup 2026: Harry Kane was surprised with a signed Brazil shirt from Ronaldo during post-match media duties. The exchange showed how elite forwards remain tied to the idols who shaped their ambitions.
FIFA World Cup 2026: Heat the new impact player at this year's tournament? A new analysis says climate change could affect playing conditions in most FIFA World Cup 2026 matches.

Sooryavanshi can revive Test cricket if he succeeds in longer format: Srikkanth Former India captain Krishnamachari Srikkanth said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has the potential to revive interest in Test cricket if he succeeds in the longest format, while urging fans and experts to give the 15-year-old time to develop.

First Singh to play in FIFA World Cup: NZ's Sarpreet wants more Indians there FIFA World Cup 2026: Sarpreet Singh said his World Cup appearance for New Zealand carried deep meaning for his family and community. His milestone as the first Sikh on this stage has widened the conversation on South Asian representation in football.

Get MS Dhoni as mentor: Sreesanth says India do not need coach Gautam GambhirFormer India fast bowler S Sreesanth has criticised head coach Gautam Gambhir's role in the national team setup, saying India would be better off with a mentor like MS Dhoni rather than a traditional coach.

The making of Shivang Kumar: SRH backing, Cummins' trust and an India dreamShivang Kumar did not even attend two Sunrisers Hyderabad trials. Yet, by the end of his debut IPL season, the left-arm wrist-spinner had become a trusted member of Pat Cummins' XI. In an exclusive interaction with IndiaToday.in, Shivang said he is in no rush to realise his India dream, choosing instead to focus on learning, improving, and making the most of the opportunities coming his way.

New season, new rival, own style: Neeraj Chopra eyes grand return in DohaNeeraj Chopra returns to action in Doha for the first time since an injury-marred eighth at last year's World Championships. The two-time Olympic medallist is fit again and chasing his own style, with Sri Lanka's Rumesh Pathirage arriving as the season's biggest threat.

Hydration or ad breaks? Who are the real winners of FIFA World Cup's new ruleFIFA's mandatory World Cup hydration breaks have split opinion among players, coaches and supporters, with some welcoming the tactical opportunities while others argue they disrupt the flow of football.

Manika Batra deeply disappointed by Asian Games snub, alleges lack of transparencyAsian Games 2026: Manika Batra has sought Prime Minister Narendra Modi's intervention after being left out of India's Asian Games table tennis squad. Her appeal has sharpened questions over transparency and the uneven application of TTFI's selection rules.

Forget the Rs 799 app: Here's how to watch FIFA World Cup in HD for just Rs 24FIFA World Cup 2026: A clever broadcast loophole lets football fans bypass expensive streaming packages and buggy apps, delivering the world's biggest tournament in flawless high-definition for less than the price of a daily cup of tea.

Harshit Rana added to India squad for third ODI against AfghanistanHarshit Rana has been added to India's squad for the third and final ODI against Afghanistan after completing his rehabilitation at the BCCI Centre of Excellence (CoE). The fast bowler has joined the team in Chennai ahead of the series finale.

Sreesanth dares Harbhajan to ring fight: Have you got the guts?Sreesanth has openly challenged Harbhajan Singh to enter the ring after taking offence at an advertisement he says referenced the 2008 slapgate episode. The outburst drags one of Indian cricket's ugliest off-field rows back into view and raises fresh questions about profiting from old humiliation.

Inspired by Virat Kohli, India's Manpreet Singh redefines longevity in hockeyModelling himself on Virat Kohli, Manpreet Singh continues to defy age in his bid to keep his LA Olympics dream alive. India's most-capped player, however, insists that his fitness will ultimately determine how long the journey continues.

Mexico edge South Korea 1-0 to become first side to qualify for last 32 stageFIFA World Cup 2026: Mexico became the first team to qualify for the Round of 32 after securing a hard-fought 1-0 win over South Korea in their Group A clash at Akron Stadium in Guadalajara on Friday.

Messi vs Ronaldo rivalry brings out the worst in fans and pundits alikeFIFA World Cup 2026: Thierry Henry's criticism of Cristiano Ronaldo reignited football's biggest divide, exposing how the Messi-Ronaldo rivalry continues to blur the line between fair analysis and personal judgement.

Scotland fans cause beer crisis in Boston during historic World Cup returnFIFA World Cup 2026: Scotland's World Cup return has sent Boston bars rushing for emergency beer deliveries. The surge has delighted local businesses and introduced the city to the Tartan Army's travelling fervour.

Kone injury takes shine off Canada's historic 6-goal World Cup rout vs QatarCanada dismantled Qatar as Jonathan David's hat-trick drove a ruthless win in Vancouver. Yet Ismael Kone's injury left Canada sweating over a key midfielder despite moving top of Group B.

Canada's Ismael Kone suffers gruesome injury, players left distraughtFIFA World Cup 2026: Canada midfielder Ismael Kone suffered a serious-looking leg injury after Assim Madibo's reckless challenge in the second half. The straight red card and the shocked reaction around the ground underlined the severity of the incident.

Taylor, Alleyne star as West Indies survive scare from spirited ScotlandStafanie Taylor's late assault and Aaliyah Alleyne's 19th-over burst carried West Indies past Scotland at Headingley. Scotland pushed a higher-ranked side deep, but missed chances and a stalled finish proved costly.

Shreyanka ruled out of T20 World Cup, India name leg-spinner as replacementShreyanka Patil has been ruled out of the Women's T20 World Cup after injuring her ankle against the Netherlands. India have brought in uncapped leg-spinner Prema Rawat as the replacement for the bowling all-rounder.

Super-sub Manzambi fires Switzerland to 4-1 win vs Bosnia and HerzegovinaFIFA World Cup 2026: Johan Manzambi came off the bench to score twice as Switzerland beat Bosnia and Herzegovina 4-1 in Los Angeles. The win put Switzerland in charge of Group B and left Bosnia under pressure ahead of Qatar.

Ivory Coast's Elye Wahi gets Canada entry despite spot-fixing probeIvory Coast forward Elye Wahi has been cleared to travel to Canada after his authorisation came through. The late approval arrived amid a French betting investigation, though he has not been named a suspect.

MEX 1-0 KOR Highlights: Mexcio qualifies for Round of 32 after edging past South KoreaMexico (MEX) vs South Korea (KOR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Mexico have completed their qualification for the round of 32 after edging past South Korea after taking the lead from a clumsy keeping effor by Korean keeper allowing Luis Romo to put it in the back of the net.

ENG vs NZ: Glenn Phillips, Matt Henry turn the screws on England at the OvalENG vs NZ, 2nd Test Day 2: Glenn Phillips scored his maiden Test century and Matt Henry struck twice as New Zealand moved ahead on day two at The Oval. England closed on 222 for six, still 169 behind and under pressure in the second Test.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
